sections.question_synthetic: What filing obligations does Retail Clerks Union Local 1442 incur when making political contributions of $300 to a local ballot measure and $100 each to local candidates in 1979?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Local 1442 will not qualify as a major donor committee requiring campaign statement filings if contributions do not total $5,000 or more in 1979. However, once they allocate $500 or more for political purposes in 1980, they will qualify as a recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a) and must file campaign statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up letter confirming prior telephone advice dated March 28, 1979. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; text is conversational. Significant portions of document are degraded or illegible due to image quality, but main legal conclusions are extractable. Government Code references are to sections 82013(a) and 82013(c) regarding committee classification thresholds.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What filing obligations does Retail Clerks Union Local 1442 incur when making political contributions of $300 to a local ballot measure and $100 each to local candidates in 1979?

CONCLUSION: Local 1442 will not qualify as a major donor committee requiring campaign statement filings if contributions do not total $5,000 or more in 1979. However, once they allocate $500 or more for political purposes in 1980, they will qualify as a recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a) and must file campaign statements.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming telephone guidance to Retail Clerks Union Local 1442 regarding filing obligations for political contributions. The letter addresses whether the union qualifies as a major donor or recipient committee based on contribution levels and filing thresholds.
